section_title: If an agent makes a contract with a person who neither knows, nor has
content:
231. If an agent makes a contract with a person who neither knows, nor has
parties to a
reason to suspect, that he is an agent, his principal may require the
contract
made by performance of the contract; but the other contracting party has, as against
agent not
the principal, the same rights as he would have had as against the agent if
disclosed
the agent had been principal.If the principal discloses himself before the
contract is completed, the other contracting party may refuse to fulfil the
contract, if he can show that, if he had known who was the principal in the

contract, or if he had known that the agent was not a principal, he would not
have entered into the contract.
Performance
